title: Fix test_package_index test
body: It started to fail a few days ago, because of some extra terminal output.

step_plan.summary: Silence stderr during package-index initialization by adding a no-op errorCallback to the loadPackage call in src/js/load-package.ts, preventing extra terminal output that caused a test to fail.

steps.operation: Pass a no-op errorCallback to loadPackage in initializePackageIndex
steps.reason: initializePackageIndex currently calls loadPackage(toLoad, { messageCallback() {} }) which suppresses normal progress messages but leaves stderr (console.error) active. Providing an errorCallback no-op (errorCallback() {}) ensures PackageManager.setCallbacks will override stderr for the duration of the initial load, preventing spurious terminal output that breaks tests.
steps.side_effects: This silences stderr output during the package-index initialization only. It will fix the failing test that asserts no extra terminal output. Potential impacts: any warnings/errors emitted during this initial load will not appear in CI logs or developer consoles, which could hide useful diagnostics; ensure no other tests rely on seeing those stderr messages. No public API changes. Downstream code behavior is unchanged outside of suppressed logging during this initialization.
